evidence_quotes.quote: Stop Killing Games also means protecting the experience of single player players.
titles designed to be self-living require constant connections, server authentication or cloud-related content.
Linking the accessibility of offline games to online services is a planned form of digital obsolescence.
Removal of online restrictions after the end of the support
Introduction of legacy modes that bypass the patch link to make games autonomous
The value of a work should not be switched off together with a server.
we support Stop Killing Games because a single player game should last as long as the passion that inspired it.

paragraphs.text: Stop Killing Games also means protecting the experience of single player players. In recent years, the boundary between offline games and online services has become increasingly blurred: titles designed to be self-living require constant connections, server authentication or cloud-related content. But what happens when these servers are switched off? Even a single player game can become inaccessible. Linking the accessibility of offline games to online services is a planned form of digital obsolescence. The MHiAP approach: It limits the player’s freedom, which depends on external infrastructure. There is a threat to the cultural preservation of the Rende games that is impossible in the long term, including for educational or archival purposes. Games should be compatible with longevity. Possible solutions include: Removal of online restrictions after the end of the support Introduction of legacy modes that bypass the patch link to make games autonomous we support Stop Killing Games because a single player game should last as long as the passion that inspired it. The value of a work should not be switched off together with a server.
